output: Implemented `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/timelineStore.ts` as a provider-scoped vanilla Zustand store with explicit `data`, `ops`, `chrome`, and `playback` slices, shallow selector hooks, safe nullable hooks gated by a mounted availability boundary, and a narrow mutable-adapter selector for the load-bearing gesture/async refs. Wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1c-from-docs-state-20260416-2053/execution_batch_3.json` and verified the new file with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.
commands_run:
pwd && git branch --show-current && rg -n "createEntityStore|zustand|shallow\)|useShallow|createStore\(|create\(" src/shared src/tools/video-editor --glob '!**/node_modules/**' && printf '
--- selectionStore ---
' && sed -n '1,260p' src/shared/state/selectionStore.ts && printf '
--- createEntityStore ---
' && sed -n '1,260p' src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts && printf '
--- useTimelineState types ---
' && sed -n '1,260p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts && printf '
--- timeline contexts ---
' && sed -n '1,280p' src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelineEditorContext.tsx && printf '
--- chrome ---
' && sed -n '1,240p' src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelineChromeContext.tsx && printf '
--- playback ---
' && sed -n '1,240p' src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelinePlaybackContext.tsx && printf '
--- useTimelineState ---
' && sed -n '1,320p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts && printf '
--- useTimelineState.contexts ---
' && sed -n '1,280p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.contexts.ts
rg -n "export type DropPosition|export interface DropPosition" src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts && sed -n '1,140p' src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts

task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented `timelineStore.ts` as a provider-scoped vanilla Zustand store so the migration can preserve a real mounted/unmounted boundary instead of falling back to a global always-available singleton. The store now carries explicit `data`, `ops`, `chrome`, and `playback` slices; shallow selector hooks for each slice; safe selector variants that return `null` unless a mounted provider is present; bootstrap/lifecycle actions (`setMounted`, `syncDataSlice`, `syncOpsSlice`, `syncChromeSlice`, `syncPlaybackSlice`, `syncSlices`, `resetSlices`); and a narrow mutable-adapter selector that exposes only the load-bearing refs needed by gesture/poll/persistence flows. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `timelineStore.ts` exposes narrow shallow-equality selector hooks for `data`, `ops`, `chrome`, and `playback`, and the safe selector path is guarded by a provider-scoped `mounted` availability boundary so nullable consumers can still distinguish `editor not mounted` from `mounted with empty/default state`.
